postingDefaults.cadence: 5-7 Shorts/week for 21 days, then 1 weekly long-form recap. Double down on whatever drives 3-second hold, comments, and shares.
approvalChecklist: Original script (no copy-paste of real Reddit posts, news articles, or copyrighted scripts)
Original or AI-generated visuals only (no movie/sports/music clips, no celebrity footage)
No fake stats, fake credentials, fabricated quotes, or unverifiable claims
No financial/medical/legal advice without clear disclaimer
No targeting of children; no kid-themed thumbnails, voices, or framing
Title and thumbnail match the actual video
Each video is meaningfully different (not a repackaged template)
Uploaded private first, reviewed in YouTube Studio, then scheduled/public

channels.thesis: Quiz and 'guess the X' formats get extreme replay, comments, and shares because viewers want to test themselves and argue in the comments. The whole format can be original scripts, AI-generated visuals, and TTS — no copyrighted footage required, and it scales to 5+ Shorts/day.
channels.targetAudience: 16-34 general audience, students, casual scrollers, trivia fans, comment-section fighters
contentPillars: guess the country from a shape
can you spot the fake fact
rank these 5 things, most people fail
sampleTopics: Only 1% can guess the country from this outline
Would you rather lose $1,000 or gain 10 years?
Rank these 5 animals by speed — most people fail #3
riskNotes: Avoid medical, financial, or 'predict the future' questions where wrong answers cause harm
No politics, no religion, no targeted groups
Original questions only — never copy a real quiz show
Each Short must add original commentary; avoid pure stock-graphic slideshows

channels.thesis: Short, weird-but-true fact explainers have proven brainrot replay value. We use fully original scripts, fact-checked sources, and AI visuals, so the channel avoids copyright strikes while still being a 'wait what?' loop that people save and share.
channels.targetAudience: 13-30 general audience, science/space/psychology fans, people who save and share 'wait what' content
formats: weekly 3-5 min 'the weirdest thing this week'
sampleTopics: This planet literally rains glass sideways
Your brain deletes most of what you see — here's why
The country that almost existed in 2025
Octopuses have 3 hearts and none of them work the way you think
The cognitive bias that makes you trust confident liars
riskNotes: Every claim must be fact-checked against a named source; never invent a statistic
Avoid scary medical content; never say 'this will happen to you'
Avoid sensationalist framing on disasters, war, or violence
No kid-themed thumbnails/voices; target curious adults and teens
